title: Silver Gore – “Black Cherry Liqueur”
author: Margaret Farrell
summary:
Last September Ava Gore and Ethan P. Flynn, aka Silver Gore, shared their great debut EP Dogs In Heaven. Earlier this week they returned with their first new music of 2026, a single called “Black Cherry Liqueur.” It’s got buzzy synths and a killer chorus; it makes existential uncertainty feel like drinking a fruity spritz.…

The post Silver Gore – “Black Cherry Liqueur” appeared first on Stereogum.
full_text:
Last September Ava Gore and Ethan P. Flynn, aka Silver Gore, shared their great debut EP Dogs In Heaven. Earlier this week they returned with their first new music of 2026, a single called "Black Cherry Liqueur." It’s got buzzy synths and a killer chorus; it makes existential uncertainty feel like drinking a fruity spritz.

Speaking about the song, Gore explained:

It’s about risk-taking, letting go and understanding that things will not turn out how you planned. It’s about being drunk on the absurdity and unexpectedness of life. But accidentally poisoning aspects of your life in doing this. Always welcoming surprise and the joy of the unexpected. Being destructive and building in the ashes.

Listen below.
